PUH-BrianMartell The Symmetry That Forces Spin-2 Emergence — Understandable Explanation The next correct step is to identify the symmetry that forces the spin-2 mode. The symmetry is emergent volume-preserving diffeomorphism invariance combined with tracelessness from the constraint phase. Simple picture: •  Volume-preserving constraint (det = 1) gives transversality (p^μ Π_μνρσ = 0) → kills vector/longitudinal modes. •  Quartic interaction + constraint phase enforces traceless alignment (η^μν Π_μνρσ = 0) → kills scalar mode. •  Together they force pure transverse-traceless projector P^{(2)} → only massless spin-2 survives. This is not assumed; it emerges dynamically from the action and E₈ symmetry. This symmetry solves the “sign problem” and the “pole problem” simultaneously — gravity is attractive tension waves protected by the symmetry.
